nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: 稀树草原
nodes.summary: 东非常见的开放草地与湿地边缘复合景观。

nodes.name: 卡尔·林奈
nodes.summary: 卡尔·林奈是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联115种鸟类，包括南方鶴鴕、角叫鴨、白臉樹鴨等115种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 戴勝屬
nodes.summary: 戴勝屬（Hoopoe，学名 Upupa epops），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，主要分布于卡尔·林奈、自然系统第十版、聖赫勒拿島戴勝，常见栖息地包括峡谷峭壁、稀树草原。

nodes.name: 峡谷峭壁
nodes.summary: 大型猛禽和食腐鸟偏好的高崖栖息环境。

nodes.name: 自然系统第十版
nodes.summary: 自然系统第十版是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联64种鸟类，包括南方鶴鴕、黑嘴樹鴨、加拿大雁等64种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 聖赫勒拿島戴勝
nodes.summary: 聖赫勒拿島戴勝是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括戴勝屬，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: | author-link=Walter Joseph Bock | year=1994 | title=History and Nomenclature of Avian Family-Group Names | series=Bulletin of the American Museum of Natural History | volume= 222 | publisher=American Museum of Natural History | location=New York | pages=146, 245 | hdl=2246/830 | url=http://digitallibrary.amnh.org/handle/2246/830 }} | display_parents = 2 | taxon = Upupa | authority = Linnaeus, 1758 | type_species = Upupa epops (Eurasian hoopoe) | type_species_authority = Linnaeus, 1758 | subdivision_ranks = Species | subdivision = *†Upupa antaios * Upupa epops * Upupa marginata }} Hoopoes () are colourful birds found across Africa, Asia, and Europe, notable for their distinctive "crown" of feathers which can be raised or lowered at will

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: | author-link=Walter Joseph Bock | year=1994 | title=History and Nomenclature of Avian Family-Group Names | series=Bulletin of the American Museum of Natural History | volume= 222 | publisher=American Museum of Natural History | location=New York | pages=146, 245 | hdl=2246/830 | url=http://digitallibrary.amnh.org/handle/2246/830 }} | display_parents = 2 | taxon = Upupa | authority = Linnaeus, 1758 | type_species = Upupa epops (Eurasian hoopoe) | type_species_authority = Linnaeus, 1758 | subdivision_ranks = Species | subdivision = *†Upupa antaios * Upupa epops * Upupa marginata }} Hoopoes () are colourful birds found across Africa, Asia, and Europe, notable for their distinctive "crown" of feathers which can be raised or lowered at will

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: |editor=Jonathan Alderfer |year=2006 |publisher=National Geographic Society |isbn=0-7922-4175-4 |page=360 }} Hoopoes have been known to breed north of their European range, and in southern England during warm, dry summers that provide plenty of grasshoppers and similar insects, although as of the early 1980s northern European populations were reported to be in the decline, possibly due to changes in climate. The hoopoe has two basic requirements of its habitat: bare or lightly vegetated ground on which to forage and vertical surfaces with cavities (such as trees, cliffs or even walls, nestboxes, haystacks, and abandoned burrows) in which to nest

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: These requirements can be provided in a wide range of ecosystems, and as a consequence the hoopoe inhabits a wide range of habitats such as heathland, wooded steppes, savannas and grasslands, as well as forest glades
